candidate_problems.text: How can 'load-bearing in practice' or 'structural entanglement' be operationalized without collapsing into a post hoc label for constitutions that survived? The conjecture needs a non-circular way to measure whether a rule is genuinely jointly constraining across institutions, coalitions, coercive capacity, fiscal extraction, and public expectations before a crisis occurs.

What is the causal relationship between constitutional entanglement and extra-constitutional power centers: do constitutions collapse because coordination has already migrated to parties, militaries, patronage networks, or foreign guarantors, or can those networks themselves be stabilized by and through constitutional rules? The unresolved tension is whether the constitution is a dependent surface expression of deeper structures or can itself reshape the operative feedback loops.

What determines the threshold at which changing underlying social problems makes formerly load-bearing provisions obsolete faster than constitutional adaptation can occur? The conjecture points to mismatch between inherited constitutional solutions and new coordination problems, but leaves open a theory of adaptation speed, modularity, and which kinds of stress can be absorbed versus which force regime replacement.
